problems.title: Coin Flip Streak
problems.notes: Classic simulation: in N experiments of 100 coin flips, how many have a streak of 6+ heads or tails? (from Automate the Boring Stuff Chapter 4). Then verify against the analytical probability.

problems.title: Dice Roll Distribution
problems.notes: Roll two fair dice 10000 times. Build a frequency table of sums. Verify that sum=7 is the most frequent. Compare empirical probabilities to the analytical formula P(sum=k). Teaches simulation vs. closed-form probability.

problems.title: Birthday Paradox Simulation
problems.notes: Run 10000 experiments: each experiment picks 23 random birthdays (1-365). Count how many experiments have at least one shared birthday. Verify it is close to 50.7%. Then extend: what group size gives 99% probability?

problems.title: Monty Hall Simulation
problems.notes: Simulate the Monty Hall problem 10000 times for both strategies (always switch vs always stay). Verify that switching wins ~2/3 of the time. Then explain WHY using conditional probability.

references.title: Counting Paths in a Grid — GeeksforGeeks

problems.title: Counting Rectangles in a Grid
problems.notes: How many rectangles (including squares) are in an m x n grid? Derive the formula using combinations: C(m+1, 2) * C(n+1, 2). Implement and verify against brute force for small grids.
